title: Self-service tenant registration with initial API key

story.i_want_to: implement a public POST /api/v1/tenants/register endpoint that creates a new tenant and its first user-role API key, returning the raw key exactly once in the response
story.so_that: new tenants can self-onboard without manual intervention, receive their admin API key immediately, and begin using the system

rationale: Self-service registration is essential for an agent-native system. AI orchestrators need to be able to programmatically create tenants and receive API keys without human intervention. The raw key is returned only at registration time - after that, only the key_prefix is visible. This is the standard pattern for API key management (similar to GitHub personal access tokens).

acceptance_criteria.description: POST /api/v1/tenants/register is a public endpoint (no authentication required)
Request body requires: name (string), slug (string), email (string). Optional: settings (object)
On success, creates a tenant with status :active and a user-role API key named 'default'
Response includes the full tenant object and the raw API key (this is the ONLY time the raw key is returned)
The entire operation (tenant creation + API key generation) is wrapped in a database transaction — if either fails, both are rolled back
Duplicate slug returns 409 Conflict with a clear error message
Invalid input (missing name, bad email, invalid slug) returns 422 with structured validation errors
Rate limiting is applied to prevent registration abuse (max 5 registrations per IP per hour)

test_cases.name: Successful tenant registration
expected_results: Response status is 201
Response body has tenant.id, tenant.name, tenant.slug, tenant.status = "active"
Response body has api_key.raw_key starting with 'lc_'
Response body has api_key.key_prefix (8 chars)
Response body has api_key.role = "user"
Tenant exists in database
API key exists in database with matching key_hash

test_cases.name: Registration with duplicate slug
expected_results: Response status is 409
Response body has error message about slug being taken
No new tenant or API key is created

test_cases.name: Registration with missing required fields
steps: POST /api/v1/tenants/register with body {"name": "Test"} (missing slug and email)
expected_results: Response status is 422
Response body has error.details with errors on slug and email

test_cases.name: Registration is transactional
steps: Mock API key generation to fail after tenant creation
expected_results: Response indicates failure
No tenant is created in the database (transaction rolled back)
No API key is created in the database

test_cases.name: Registration does not require authentication
expected_results: Response is not 401 (endpoint is public)
Registration proceeds normally

test_cases.name: Returned API key can authenticate subsequent requests
steps: POST /api/v1/tenants/register and capture raw_key from response
expected_results: Second request returns 200 with the tenant profile
The returned tenant matches the one created during registration

test_cases.name: Registration with custom settings
expected_results: Response status is 201
Tenant settings include the provided timezone value

technical_notes: Use Ecto.Multi for the transactional tenant + API key creation
The endpoint must be outside the :authenticated pipeline in the router
Rate limiting on this endpoint can use a simple IP-based ETS counter (or defer to US-2.7)
The response JSON view should have a special registration_json/1 that includes the raw_key (not used in any other API key view)
Consider logging registration events to the audit trail with actor_type: 'system'
GET /api/v1/tenants/me and PATCH /api/v1/tenants/me are referenced here but will be implemented as part of this story since they are simple tenant profile endpoints
